functions.description: Stage 1 — enrich every span with sampling-decision metadata. duration_ms from start/end nanos. is_error: OTel status.code=2 OR HTTP 5xx OR non-zero gRPC. is_slow: duration > 1000ms. service_name: extracted from resource.attributes['service.name']. trace_bucket: parseInt of the last 2 hex chars of trace_id (uses substring(30,32) to avoid the negative-index slice issue that Cribl rejects silently). keep_reason: errors and slow always kept; ~10% deterministic baseline kept (26/256 of trace_bucket values); everything else dropped.

SAMPLING TOGGLE — drops spans whose keep_reason is 'drop'. Errors, slow traces (>1000ms), and ~10% deterministic baseline (trace_bucket < 26 out of 0-255) are kept. Deterministic per trace_id, so all spans of the same trace share the same fate — no partial traces in Honeycomb. Disabled by default for safety. Lake retention unaffected — the route to Lake forks before this pipeline runs.

conf.description: Trace pre-filtering for cost control. Two functions: Eval (enrich every span with is_error / is_slow / trace_bucket / keep_reason) and Drop (toggle to actually drop). Complements Refinery downstream; Lake retains 100% via separate route. Simplified version without CMDB lookup — pure deterministic sampling. In production, layer in a Lookup function before this Eval to enrich with criticality_tier, slo_target_ms, oncall_team, biz_unit etc. for tier-aware decisions.
